# servicenow_data_lookup (Resource)

`servicenow_data_lookup` manages a data lookup definition (dl_definition) within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a data lookup definition (dl_definition) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_data_lookup" "example" {
  name           = "Incident Priority Lookup"
  table          = "incident"
  lookup_table   = "dl_u_priority"
  matcher_fields = "impact,urgency"
  setter_fields  = "priority"
  active         = true
  description    = "Sets incident priority based on impact and urgency."
  order          = 100
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `lookup_table` (String) Source table for the lookup values (maps to ServiceNow's `matcher_table` column).
- `name` (String) Name of the data lookup definition.
- `table` (String) Table that the lookup applies to (maps to ServiceNow's `source_table` column).

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether or not this data lookup is enabled.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `run_on_form_change` (Boolean) If true, the lookup runs from the client form on field changes.
- `run_on_insert` (Boolean) If true, the lookup runs when a target record is inserted.
- `run_on_update` (Boolean) If true, the lookup runs when a target record is updated.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_data_lookup.example <sys_id>
```
